title: Incorrect result from sqlite3_stmt_status with SQLITE_STMTSTATUS_FULLSCAN_STEP
description:
drh added on 2013-06-14 13:52:01:
The sqlite3_stmt_status() interface with the 
SQLITE_STMTSTATUS_FULLSCAN_STEP parameter can sometimes return zero when
in fact a full scan of an index is undertaken.  The problem arises when
a full scan of an index is used in place of a full table scan in order to
fulfill an ORDER BY clause without sorting.
The problem is fixed in the NGQP.  The problem was noticed when comparing
results between NGQP and the legacy query planner.  This ticket is merely to
document the problem.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name NVARCHAR(255) NOT NULL, age INTEGER, created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO test_table (name, age) VALUES ('Alice', 30), ('Bob', NULL), ('Charlie', 25);
SELECT * FROM test_table ORDER BY name;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_table WHERE name IS NOT NULL;
